tasks.title: Mow active spring growth
tasks.timing: April 19-24, 2026 if lawn is actively growing and tall enough
weatherRules: Best before windy or stormy stretches.
tasks.notes: Keep tall fescue on the taller side and do not remove more than about one-third of the blade.

tasks.title: Confirm crabgrass pre-emergent status
tasks.leadTime: Buy in February or March if not already on hand
tasks.recurrence: Annual if crabgrass prevention is desired
tasks.notes: Use only if crabgrass has been a real issue or prevention is desired this year.

tasks.title: Spot check weeds and thin areas
tasks.leadTime: No major purchase needed unless weeds are significant
weatherRules: Spot-treat only when label conditions are right.
tasks.notes: Mark thin or bare areas for fall seeding rather than forcing a spring renovation.

tasks.title: Plan pre-emergent purchase
tasks.notes: Use the 8,225 sq ft lawn total for purchase planning. A 5,000 sq ft unit covers about 61% of the lawn, so two units are typically needed for full-property coverage.

tasks.title: Decide whether spring feeding is worth it
tasks.timing: May only if lawn is actively growing and summer watering is realistic
tasks.leadTime: Confirm irrigation ability and whether the product fits the season before applying
weatherRules: Avoid pushing growth before hot/dry periods.
Follow product label for temperature, rain, and watering directions.
tasks.notes: Heavy early spring nitrogen can increase mowing, weeds, and disease pressure.

tasks.title: Irrigation startup and backflow check
tasks.timing: Spring after meaningful freeze risk has passed
tasks.leadTime: Schedule or inspect before heat-driven watering matters
weatherRules: Delay startup if hard freeze risk remains.
tasks.notes: This is a property system task, not just a lawn task.

tasks.title: Check freeze risk before startup
tasks.timing: Before sprinkler startup or tender spring work
weatherRules: Delay startup if hard freeze risk remains.
tasks.notes: This is the weather-aware dependency for irrigation startup and other cold-sensitive work.

tasks.title: Check irrigation coverage
tasks.leadTime: Run a catch-can or rain-gauge test before heat stress decisions matter
weatherRules: Use recent rain and soil moisture to decide whether a test is useful now.
tasks.notes: Coverage data should inform watering, fertilizer, and dry-zone decisions.

tasks.title: Review mulch cycle
tasks.timing: Spring review; every-other-year refresh if beds need it
tasks.leadTime: Plan quantity and delivery/pickup before peak spring rush
tasks.recurrence: Every other year or as beds require
tasks.notes: Purchase history shows 120 bags of premium dark brown mulch on April 1, 2025.

tasks.title: Prepare for fall seed work
tasks.timing: Late July-August planning for September work
tasks.leadTime: Buy seed and fertilizer before September
tasks.recurrence: Annual review; apply only where needed
weatherRules: Seed when temperatures and irrigation plan support establishment.
tasks.notes: Existing purchase history includes one 2.4 lb tall fescue mix bag from March 18, 2025.

tasks.title: Apply late fall nitrogen
tasks.leadTime: Buy fall fertilizer before the November window; two 5,000 sq ft units are the full-property planning assumption
weatherRules: Water in if rain does not.
tasks.notes: This is usually higher value for tall fescue than pushing summer color. Treat Scotts WinterGuard product code 50240 as a 5,000 sq ft working product.
